{{神社| 名称=和霊神社| 所在地=愛媛県宇和島市和霊町| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|47|N|132|33|55|E|}}| 祭神=山部清兵衛公頼公| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]）| 例祭=[[7月23日]]・[[7月24日|24日]]（和霊大祭）}}
'''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。
[[宇和島藩]]家老・[[山部公頼]]（やんべきみより。通称 清兵衛）を主[[祭神]]とする。公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[元和]]元年（[[1615年]]）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、その家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、元和6年、藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼とその息子らを殺害させた。
公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死し、また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。[[享保]]20年（[[1735年]]）に現在地の鎌江城跡に遷座した。大正8年に県社に列した。昭和28年（[[1953年]]）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。
7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は和霊大祭と呼ばれる。
当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。
[[Category:愛媛県の神社|われいしんしや]] [[Category:府県社|われいしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|われいしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

拝殿}} {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 沿革 == 宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。 [[宇和島藩]]家老・[[山家公頼]]（やんべきんより。通称 清兵衛）を主[[祭神]]とする。公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1615年]]）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、その家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、元和6年、藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼とその息子らを殺害させた（[[和霊騒動]]）。 公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死し、また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。[[享保]]20年（[[1735年]]）に現在地に遷座した。[[大正]]8年（[[1919年]]）に県社に列した。[[昭和]]28年（[[1953年]]）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる。 当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:宇和島市]] {{shinto-stub}}

拝殿}} {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 沿革 == 宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。 [[宇和島藩]]家老・[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛）を主[[祭神]]とする。公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1615年]]）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、その家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、元和6年、藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼とその息子らを殺害させた（[[和霊騒動]]）。 公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死し、また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。[[享保]]20年（[[1735年]]）に現在地に遷座した。[[大正]]8年（[[1919年]]）に県社に列した。[[昭和]]28年（[[1953年]]）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）に空襲で焼失されたが、昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に見事再建された。 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}。 7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる。 当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 == 関連項目 == * [[牛鬼]] * [[丸之内和霊神社]] * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == *[http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html 宇和島市観光協会（和霊神社）] *[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ] *[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）] *[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:宇和島市の歴史]] [[Category:宇和島藩]] [[Category:山家氏]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:宇和島市の建築物]]

revision.comment: 神社テンプレートに沿った修正
revision.text:
{{画像提供依頼|戦前の随神門|戦前の社殿|date=2016年9月|cat=宇和島市|cat2=歴史}} {{神社 |名称 = 和霊神社 |画像 = [[File:Warei Jinja 04.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 13 | 緯度秒 = 47 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 33 | 経度秒 = 55 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Ehime | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[山家公頼|山家清兵衛公頼公]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|県社]]別表神社 |創建 = [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =[[7月23日]]・[[7月24日|24日]]（和霊大祭） |神事 = }} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛） :公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、[[宇和島藩]]家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、1620年（元和6年）に藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼はその息子らとともに殺害された（[[和霊騒動]]）。 == 沿革 == 神社は宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死するなど異変が宇和島に巻き起こった。 また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[1653年]]（[[承応]]2年）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。その後、参拝者が増大し社域が狭隘になったことから社地は向山（[[宇和島市立城北中学校]]内）、次いで森安へと移った[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。さらに第四代藩主である[[伊達村年]]治世の[[1731年]]（[[享保]]16年）3月27日には、「和霊社勧化を以造営在之ニ付、小関兵右衛門右奉行被仰付候事」となり鎌江城跡の一角に社地が再び移されることとなり、[[1735年]]（[[享保]]20年）に現在地に遷座した。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に県社に列した。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1945年]]（昭和20年）に空襲で焼失されたが、[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に見事再建された。当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 == 境内 == [[社叢]]は1974年に市の天然記念物に指定される[https://www.city.uwajima.ehime.jp/site/sizen-bunka/155warei.html 宇和島市のホームページ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 祭事 == 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}。7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[四国旅客鉄道|JR四国]][[予讃線]][[宇和島駅]]下車後、徒歩約7分 == 関連項目 == * [[和霊神社（高知市）]] - 高知県高知市にある神社。坂本龍馬の4代前の先祖は和霊神社から勧請した[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 * [[丸之内和霊神社]] - 公頼公の邸宅跡にある神社。 * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} *[http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html 宇和島市観光協会（和霊神社）] *[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 和霊神社 - 愛媛県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇和島市の建築物]] [[Category:宇和島市の歴史]] [[Category:宇和島藩]] [[Category:山家氏]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{画像提供依頼|戦前の随神門|戦前の社殿|date=2016年9月|cat=宇和島市|cat2=歴史}} 、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。
==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛） :公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、[[宇和島藩]]家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、1620年（元和6年）に藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼はその息子らとともに殺害された（[[和霊騒動]]）。
== 沿革 == 神社は宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死するなど異変が宇和島に巻き起こった。
また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[1653年]]（[[承応]]2年）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。その後、参拝者が増大し社域が狭隘になったことから社地は向山（[[宇和島市立城北中学校]]内）、次いで森安へと移った<ref name="和霊信仰">[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）]2019年6月17日 閲覧</ref>。さらに第四代藩主である[[伊達村年]]治世の[[1731年]]（[[享保]]16年）3月27日には、「和霊社勧化を以造営在之ニ付、小関兵右衛門右奉行被仰付候事」となり鎌江城跡の一角に社地が再び移されることとなり、[[1735年]]（[[享保]]20年）に現在地に遷座した<ref name="和霊信仰" />。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に県社に列した。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。
[[1945年]]（昭和20年）に空襲で焼失されたが、[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に見事再建された。当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。
== 境内 == [[社叢]]は1974年に市の天然記念物に指定される<ref>[https://www.city.uwajima.ehime.jp/site/sizen-bunka/155warei.html 宇和島市のホームページ]2019年6月17日 閲覧</ref>。
== 祭事 == 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる<ref>{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}</ref>。7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる<ref name="和霊大祭">[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）]2019年6月17日 閲覧</ref>。

拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 13 | 緯度秒 = 47 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 33 | 経度秒 = 55 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Ehime | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[山家公頼|山家清兵衛公頼公]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|県社]]別表神社 |創建 = [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =[[7月23日]]・[[7月24日|24日]]（和霊大祭） |神事 = }} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛） :公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、[[宇和島藩]]家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、1620年（元和6年）に藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼はその息子らとともに殺害された（[[和霊騒動]]）。 == 沿革 == 神社は宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死するなど異変が宇和島に巻き起こった。 また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[1653年]]（[[承応]]2年）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。その後、参拝者が増大し社域が狭隘になったことから社地は向山（[[宇和島市立城北中学校]]内）、次いで森安へと移った[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。さらに第四代藩主である[[伊達村年]]治世の[[1731年]]（[[享保]]16年）3月27日には、「和霊社勧化を以造営在之ニ付、小関兵右衛門右奉行被仰付候事」となり鎌江城跡の一角に社地が再び移されることとなり、[[1735年]]（[[享保]]20年）に現在地に遷座した。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に県社に列した。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1945年]]（昭和20年）に空襲で焼失されたが、[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に見事再建された。当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 == 境内 == [[社叢]]は1974年に市の天然記念物に指定される[https://www.city.uwajima.ehime.jp/site/sizen-bunka/155warei.html 宇和島市のホームページ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 祭事 == 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}。7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[四国旅客鉄道|JR四国]][[予讃線]][[宇和島駅]]下車後、徒歩約7分 == ロケ地として == * 『[[南海の狼火]]』(1960年、日活映画) 主演[[小林旭]]{{cite web|url=https://www.nikkatsu.com/movie/20476.html|title=南海の狼火|publisher=日活|access-date=2022-8-15}} == 関連項目 == * [[和霊神社（高知市）]] - 高知県高知市にある神社。坂本龍馬の4代前の先祖は和霊神社から勧請した[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 * [[丸之内和霊神社]] - 公頼公の邸宅跡にある神社。 * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} *[http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html 宇和島市観光協会（和霊神社）] *[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 和霊神社 - 愛媛県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇和島市の建築物]] [[Category:宇和島市の歴史]] [[Category:宇和島藩]] [[Category:山家氏]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{画像提供依頼|戦前の随神門|戦前の社殿|date=2016年9月|cat=宇和島市|cat2=歴史}} {{神社 |名称 = 和霊神社 |画像 = [[File:Warei Jinja 04.JPG|250px]]

[[山家公頼|山家清兵衛公頼公]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|県社]]別表神社 |創建 = [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =[[7月23日]]・[[7月24日|24日]]（和霊大祭） |神事 = }} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛） :公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、[[宇和島藩]]家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、1620年（元和6年）に藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼はその息子らとともに殺害された（[[和霊騒動]]）。 == 沿革 == 神社は宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死するなど異変が宇和島に巻き起こった。 また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[1653年]]（[[承応]]2年）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。その後、参拝者が増大し社域が狭隘になったことから社地は向山（[[宇和島市立城北中学校]]内）、次いで森安へと移った[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。さらに第四代藩主である[[伊達村年]]治世の[[1731年]]（[[享保]]16年）3月27日には、「和霊社勧化を以造営在之ニ付、小関兵右衛門右奉行被仰付候事」となり鎌江城跡の一角に社地が再び移されることとなり、[[1735年]]（[[享保]]20年）に現在地に遷座した。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に県社に列した。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1945年]]（昭和20年）に空襲で焼失されたが、[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に見事再建された。当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 == 境内 == [[社叢]]は1974年に市の天然記念物に指定される[https://www.city.uwajima.ehime.jp/site/sizen-bunka/155warei.html 宇和島市のホームページ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 祭事 == 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}。7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[四国旅客鉄道|JR四国]][[予讃線]][[宇和島駅]]下車後、徒歩約7分 == ロケ地として == * 『[[南海の狼火]]』(1960年、日活映画) 主演[[小林旭]]{{cite web|url=https://www.nikkatsu.com/movie/20476.html|title=南海の狼火|publisher=日活|access-date=2022-8-15}} == 仙台との繋がり == 山家清兵衛公頼が[[仙台藩|仙台]]に残した長男・喜兵衛の子孫である山家豊三郎（頼道）は、宇和島の和霊神社からの[[分霊]]を「山家明神」として東一番丁の自邸に祀っていた。 その後、「山家明神」の号を「和霊神社」と改め、明治16年（[[1883年]]）には屋敷の移転に伴い、[[東二番丁通り|東二番丁]]と[[立町 (仙台市)|立町]]通り角附近の屋敷地内に遷座した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）7月の[[仙台空襲]]では、狛犬2体を残して全焼。 昭和48年（[[1973年]]）には山家家の移転とともに台原（現在の[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]台原）に遷座した。 昭和52年（[[1977年]]）、かつて豊三郎が屋敷を構え「山家明神」を祀っていた東一番丁を前身に持つ[[一番町 (仙台市)|一番町一番街商店街]]から、さらなる活性化の祈願と町の守護神としてふたたび和霊神社を、という声が高まり、ジャスコ（現在の[[仙台フォーラス]]）の屋上に和霊神社を分霊・遷座することとなった。毎年7月には「一番町三社まつり」の一社として、[[藤崎 (百貨店)|藤崎]]えびす神社、野中神社の[[神輿]]とともに和霊神社の神輿が仙台市の中心部商店街を渡御する一番町一番街商店街振興組合『和霊神社の由来　一番町ものがたり』より。。 == 関連項目 == * [[和霊神社（高知市）]] - 高知県高知市にある神社。坂本龍馬の4代前の先祖は和霊神社から勧請した[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 * [[丸之内和霊神社]] - 公頼公の邸宅跡にある神社。 * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == (百貨店)|藤崎]]えびす神社、野中神社の[[神輿]]とともに和霊神社の神輿が仙台市の中心部商店街を渡御する一番町一番街商店街振興組合『和霊神社の由来　一番町ものがたり』より。。 == 関連項目 == * [[和霊神社（高知市）]] - 高知県高知市にある神社。坂本龍馬の4代前の先祖は和霊神社から勧請した[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 * [[丸之内和霊神社]] - 公頼公の邸宅跡にある神社。 * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == {{画像提供依頼|戦前の随神門|戦前の社殿|date=2016年9月|cat=宇和島市|cat2=歴史}} {{神社 |名称 = 和霊神社 |画像 = [[File:Warei Jinja 04.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 13 | 緯度秒 = 47 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 33 | 経度秒 = 55 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Ehime | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[山家公頼|山家清兵衛公頼公]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|県社]]別表神社 |創建 = [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =[[7月23日]]・[[7月24日|24日]]（和霊大祭） |神事 = }} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛） :公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、[[宇和島藩]]家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、1620年（元和6年）に藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼はその息子らとともに殺害された（[[和霊騒動]]）。 == 沿革 == 神社は宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死するなど異変が宇和島に巻き起こった。 また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[1653年]]（[[承応]]2年）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。その後、参拝者が増大し社域が狭隘になったことから社地は向山（[[宇和島市立城北中学校]]内）、次いで森安へと移った[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。さらに第四代藩主である[[伊達村年]]治世の[[1731年]]（[[享保]]16年）3月27日には、「和霊社勧化を以造営在之ニ付、小関兵右衛門右奉行被仰付候事」となり鎌江城跡の一角に社地が再び移されることとなり、[[1735年]]（[[享保]]20年）に現在地に遷座した。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に県社に列した。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1945年]]（昭和20年）に空襲で焼失されたが、[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に見事再建された。当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 == 境内 == [[社叢]]は1974年に市の天然記念物に指定される[https://www.city.uwajima.ehime.jp/site/sizen-bunka/155warei.html 宇和島市のホームページ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 祭事 == 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}。7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[四国旅客鉄道|JR四国]][[予讃線]][[宇和島駅]]下車後、徒歩約7分 == ロケ地として == * 『[[南海の狼火]]』(1960年、日活映画) 主演[[小林旭]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nikkatsu.com/movie/20476.html|title=南海の狼火|publisher=日活|access-date=2022-8-15}} == 仙台との繋がり == 山家清兵衛公頼が[[仙台藩|仙台]]に残した長男・喜兵衛の子孫である山家豊三郎（頼道）は、宇和島の和霊神社からの[[分霊]]を「山家明神」として東一番丁の自邸に祀っていた。 その後、「山家明神」の号を「和霊神社」と改め、明治16年（[[1883年]]）には屋敷の移転に伴い、[[東二番丁通り|東二番丁]]と[[立町 (仙台市)|立町]]通り角附近の屋敷地内に遷座した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）7月の[[仙台空襲]]では、狛犬2体を残して全焼。 昭和48年（[[1973年]]）には山家家の移転とともに台原（現在の[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]台原）に遷座した。 昭和52年（[[1977年]]）、かつて豊三郎が屋敷を構え「山家明神」を祀っていた東一番丁を前身に持つ[[一番町 (仙台市)|一番町一番街商店街]]から、さらなる活性化の祈願と町の守護神としてふたたび和霊神社を、という声が高まり、ジャスコ（現在の[[仙台フォーラス]]）の屋上に和霊神社を分霊・遷座することとなった。毎年7月には「一番町三社まつり」の一社として、[[藤崎 (百貨店)|藤崎]]えびす神社、野中神社の[[神輿]]とともに和霊神社の神輿が仙台市の中心部商店街を渡御する一番町一番街商店街振興組合『和霊神社の由来　一番町ものがたり』より。。 == 和霊信仰 == [[中四国]]を中心に信仰されている。[[1982年]]現在の分社数は、小規模社や境内末社等を合わせて[[西日本]]10府県で159社という。勧請年が早いものとして、[[高知県]][[四万十町]]六反地の和霊神社（1737年）と、愛媛県[[今治市]][[玉川町]]法界寺の和霊神社（1746年）がある。 前社は妻スミが亡くなった地という言い伝えがあり、公頼と妻スミが併せて祀られている。後社は、[[伊予国]]一の宮の[[大山祗神社]]の神官に和霊神社に参拝すると良いと言われ、庄屋浮穴与右衛門包俊がその通りにすると病気が全快したことによる勧請である。当初は法界寺[[三島神社]]（大山祗神社の分社）の境内社であったが、参拝者が増えたため独立したものという。『臨海都市圏の生活文化』（和霊信仰の広がり）『愛媛県史　民俗　上』（三島信仰と和霊信仰） == 関連項目 == * [[和霊神社（高知市）]] - 高知県高知市にある神社。坂本龍馬の4代前の先祖は和霊神社から勧請した[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 * [[丸之内和霊神社]] - 公頼公の邸宅跡にある神社。 * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} *[http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html 宇和島市観光協会（和霊神社）] *[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 和霊神社 - 愛媛県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇和島市の建築物]] [[Category:宇和島市の歴史]] {{画像提供依頼|戦前の随神門|戦前の社殿|date=2016年9月|cat=宇和島市|cat2=歴史}} {{神社 |名称 = 和霊神社 |画像 = [[File:Warei Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 13 | 緯度秒 = 47 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 33 | 経度秒 = 55 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Ehime | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[山家公頼|山家清兵衛公頼公]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|県社]]別表神社 |創建 = [[承応]]2年（[[1653年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 =[[7月23日]]・[[7月24日|24日]]（和霊大祭） |神事 = }} '''和霊神社'''（われいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== ;主祭神 *[[山家公頼]]（通称 清兵衛） :公頼は[[伊達政宗]]の家臣であったが、[[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）政宗の長男・[[伊達秀宗|秀宗]]が宇和島に移封されるのに従い、[[宇和島藩]]家老として藩政を支えた。宇和島はそれまでの領主の悪政により疲弊していたが、公頼は租税軽減や産業振興を行い、効果を上げた。しかし、1620年（元和6年）に藩主秀宗は公頼を嫉妬する藩士による讒言を信じ、公頼はその息子らとともに殺害された（[[和霊騒動]]）。 == 沿革 == 神社は宇和島市街地の北端、鎌江城跡に鎮座する。公頼を慕う領民たちは、密かに城北森安の八面荒神の境内に小祠を設けて公頼一族の霊を祀った。その後、公頼殺害に関与した者が落雷・海難などにより次々と変死するなど異変が宇和島に巻き起こった。 また公頼の無実も判明したため、[[1653年]]（[[承応]]2年）、秀宗は公頼を祀る神社を創建し、山頼和霊神社と称した。その後、参拝者が増大し社域が狭隘になったことから社地は向山（[[宇和島市立城北中学校]]内）、次いで森安へと移った[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:2/49/view/6545 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊信仰）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。さらに第四代藩主である[[伊達村年]]治世の[[1731年]]（[[享保]]16年）3月27日には、「和霊社勧化を以造営在之ニ付、小関兵右衛門右奉行被仰付候事」となり鎌江城跡の一角に社地が再び移されることとなり、[[1735年]]（[[享保]]20年）に現在地に遷座した。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に県社に列した。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1945年]]（昭和20年）に空襲で焼失されたが、[[1957年]]（昭和32年）に見事再建された。当社の分霊を祀る神社が、四国・中国地方を中心に日本各地にある。 == 境内 == [[社叢]]は1974年に市の天然記念物に指定される[https://www.city.uwajima.ehime.jp/site/sizen-bunka/155warei.html 宇和島市のホームページ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 祭事 == 1月2日・3日には刀剣武術の居合いの奉納が行われる{{Cite journal ja-jp|title=サライの『神社大全』|journal=[[サライ (雑誌)|サライ]]|serial=2011年1月号|publisher=[[小学館]]|pages=71p}}。7月23日・24日の[[例祭]]は[[和霊大祭]]と呼ばれる[http://www.i-manabi.jp/system/regionals/regionals/ecode:1/5/view/1010 愛媛県生涯学習センター・データベース『愛媛の記憶』（和霊大祭）]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[愛媛県]][[宇和島市]]和霊町1451 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[四国旅客鉄道|JR四国]][[予讃線]][[宇和島駅]]下車後、徒歩約7分 == ロケ地として == * 『[[南海の狼火]]』(1960年、日活映画) 主演[[小林旭]]{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nikkatsu.com/movie/20476.html|title=南海の狼火|publisher=日活|access-date=2022-8-15}} == 仙台との繋がり == 山家清兵衛公頼が[[仙台藩|仙台]]に残した長男・喜兵衛の子孫である山家豊三郎（頼道）は、宇和島の和霊神社からの[[分霊]]を「山家明神」として東一番丁の自邸に祀っていた。 その後、「山家明神」の号を「和霊神社」と改め、明治16年（[[1883年]]）には屋敷の移転に伴い、[[東二番丁通り|東二番丁]]と[[立町 (仙台市)|立町]]通り角附近の屋敷地内に遷座した。 昭和20年（[[1945年]]）7月の[[仙台空襲]]では、狛犬2体を残して全焼。 昭和48年（[[1973年]]）には山家家の移転とともに台原（現在の[[青葉区 (仙台市)|青葉区]]台原）に遷座した。 昭和52年（[[1977年]]）、かつて豊三郎が屋敷を構え「山家明神」を祀っていた東一番丁を前身に持つ[[一番町 (仙台市)|一番町一番街商店街]]から、さらなる活性化の祈願と町の守護神としてふたたび和霊神社を、という声が高まり、ジャスコ（現在の[[仙台フォーラス]]）の屋上に和霊神社を分霊・遷座することとなった。毎年7月には「一番町三社まつり」の一社として、[[藤崎 (百貨店)|藤崎]]えびす神社、野中神社の[[神輿]]とともに和霊神社の神輿が仙台市の中心部商店街を渡御する一番町一番街商店街振興組合『和霊神社の由来　一番町ものがたり』より。。 == 和霊信仰 == [[中四国]]を中心に信仰されている。[[1982年]]現在の分社数は、小規模社や境内末社等を合わせて[[西日本]]と9府県（愛媛県82社、岡山県26社等）と東北1県（仙台市2社）で159社という。勧請年が早いものとして、[[高知県]][[四万十町]]六反地の和霊神社（1737年）と、愛媛県[[今治市]][[玉川町]]法界寺の和霊神社（1746年）がある。 前社は妻スミが亡くなった地という言い伝えがあり、公頼と妻スミが併せて祀られている。後社は、[[伊予国]]一の宮の[[大山祗神社]]の神官に和霊神社に参拝すると良いと言われ、庄屋浮穴与右衛門包俊がその通りにすると病気が全快したことによる勧請である。当初は法界寺[[三島神社]]（大山祗神社の分社）の境内社であったが、参拝者が増えたため独立したものという。『臨海都市圏の生活文化』（和霊信仰の広がり）『愛媛県史　民俗　上』（三島信仰と和霊信仰） == 関連項目 == * [[和霊神社（高知市）]] - 高知県高知市にある神社。坂本龍馬の4代前の先祖は和霊神社から勧請した[http://ryomadappansai.blog96.fc2.com/blog-entry-1.html 和霊神社と龍馬脱藩祭 ]2019年6月17日　閲覧。 * [[丸之内和霊神社]] - 公頼公の邸宅跡にある神社。 * [[明石虎雄]] - 地元・宇和島出身の建築家。参籠所を設計、監督、施工した。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} *[http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html 宇和島市観光協会（和霊神社）] *[http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 和霊神社 - 愛媛県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇和島市の建築物]] [[Category:宇和島市の歴史]] [[Category:宇和島藩]] [[Category:山家氏]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:別表神社]]

revision.comment: 曖昧さ回避ページ[[玉川町]]へのリンクを解消、リンク先を[[玉川町 (愛媛県)]]に変更([[User:ネイ/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]]使用)

{{Image request|Pre-war Zuishinmon|Pre-war main shrine|date=September 2016|cat=Uwajima City|cat2=History}} {{Shrine |Name=Warei Shrine |Image=[[File:Warei Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 |Latitude degrees=33 | Latitude minutes=13 | Latitude seconds=47 | N/S=N |Longitude degrees=132 |Longitude minutes=33 |Longitude seconds=55 | E/W=E |Country code=JP |Map=Japan Ehime |Label position=left |Deity={{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼|lt=Yamaga Seibei Kiyomasa}} |Rank=Old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} Prefectural Shrine, {{ill|Special Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |Founded={{ill|Jōō|ja|承応}} 2 (1653) |Main hall= |Other names= |Annual festival={{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}}・{{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}} (Warei Festival) |Ceremonies= }} '''Warei Shrine'''（われいじんじゃ{{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. In 1615 (Genna 1), he followed Masamune's eldest son, {{ill|Date Hidemune|ja|伊達秀宗}}, when he was transferred to Uwajima. As a chief retainer of the Uwajima Domain, he supported the domain's administration. Uwajima had been impoverished by the previous lord's misrule, but Kiyomasa implemented tax reductions and industrial promotion, which had a positive effect. However, in 1620 (Genna 6), Hidemune, influenced by slander from other retainers, believed the accusations and had Kiyomasa and his sons killed ({{ill|Warei Incident|ja|和霊騒動}}). == History == The shrine is located at the northern end of Uwajima city, on the site of Kamegae Castle. The people who admired Kiyomasa secretly built a small shrine in the grounds of Yamen Koshin Shrine in Kitamori, where they enshrined the spirits of Kiyomasa and his family. Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. When it became clear that Kiyomasa was innocent, Hidemune established a shrine dedicated to Kiyomasa in 1653 (Jōō 2) and named it Yamayorikawa Rei Shrine. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1731 (Kyōhō 16), the fourth domain lord {{ill|Date Muratoki|ja|伊達村年}} ordered the shrine to be moved again to a part of the Kamegae Castle ruins, and it was relocated to its present site in 1735 (Kyōhō 20). In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == Ceremonies == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei Festival. == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming Location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines Festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Special Shrines]]

It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]]

{{Image request|Pre-war Zuishinmon|Pre-war main shrine|date=September 2016|cat=Uwajima City|cat2=History}} {{Shrine |Name=Warei Shrine |Image=[[File:Warei Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 |Latitude degrees=33 | Latitude minutes=13 | Latitude seconds=47 | N/S=N |Longitude degrees=132 |Longitude minutes=33 |Longitude seconds=55 | E/W=E |Country code=JP |Map=Japan Ehime |Label position=left |Deity={{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼|lt=Yamaga Seibei Kiyomasa}} |Rank=Old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} Prefectural Shrine, {{ill|Special Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |Founded={{ill|Jōō|ja|承応}} 2 (1653) |Main hall= |Other names= |Annual festival={{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}}・{{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}} (Warei Festival) |Ceremonies= }} '''Warei Shrine'''（われいじんじゃ{{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. In 1615 (Genna 1), he followed Masamune's eldest son, {{ill|Date Hidemune|ja|伊達秀宗}}, when he was transferred to Uwajima. As a chief retainer of the Uwajima Domain, he supported the domain's administration. Uwajima had been impoverished by the previous lord's misrule, but Kiyomasa implemented tax reductions and industrial promotion, which had a positive effect. However, in 1620 (Genna 6), Hidemune, influenced by slander from other retainers, believed the accusations and had Kiyomasa and his sons killed ({{ill|Warei Incident|ja|和霊騒動}}). == History == The shrine is located at the northern end of Uwajima city, on the site of Kamegae Castle. The people who admired Kiyomasa secretly built a small shrine in the grounds of Yamen Koshin Shrine in Kitamori, where they enshrined the spirits of Kiyomasa and his family. Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. When it became clear that Kiyomasa was innocent, Hidemune established a shrine dedicated to Kiyomasa in 1653 (Jōō 2) and named it Yamayorikawa Rei Shrine. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1731 (Kyōhō 16), the fourth domain lord {{ill|Date Muratoki|ja|伊達村年}} ordered the shrine to be moved again to a part of the Kamegae Castle ruins, and it was relocated to its present site in 1735 (Kyōhō 20). In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == Ceremonies == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei Festival. == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming Location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines Festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

{{Image request|Pre-war Zuishinmon|Pre-war main shrine|date=September 2016|cat=Uwajima City|cat2=History}} {{Shrine |Name=Warei Shrine |Image=[[File:Warei Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall |Location={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 |Latitude degrees=33 | Latitude minutes=13 | Latitude seconds=47 | N/S=N |Longitude degrees=132 |Longitude minutes=33 |Longitude seconds=55 | E/W=E |Country code=JP |Map=Japan Ehime |Label position=left |Deity={{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼|lt=Yamaga Seibei Kiyomasa}} |Rank=Old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} Prefectural Shrine, {{ill|Special Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |Founded={{ill|Jōō|ja|承応}} 2 (1653) |Main hall= |Other names= |Annual festival={{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}}・{{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}} (Warei Festival) |Ceremonies= }} '''Warei Shrine'''（われいじんじゃ{{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. In 1615 (Genna 1), he followed Masamune's eldest son, {{ill|Date Hidemune|ja|伊達秀宗}}, when he was transferred to Uwajima. As a chief retainer of the Uwajima Domain, he supported the domain's administration. Uwajima had been impoverished by the previous lord's misrule, but Kiyomasa implemented tax reductions and industrial promotion, which had a positive effect. However, in 1620 (Genna 6), Hidemune, influenced by slander from other retainers, believed the accusations and had Kiyomasa and his sons killed ({{ill|Warei Incident|ja|和霊騒動}}). == History == The shrine is located at the northern end of Uwajima city, on the site of Kamegae Castle. The people who admired Kiyomasa secretly built a small shrine in the grounds of Yamen Koshin Shrine in Kitamori, where they enshrined the spirits of Kiyomasa and his family. Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. When it became clear that Kiyomasa was innocent, Hidemune established a shrine dedicated to Kiyomasa in 1653 (Jōō 2) and named it Yamayorikawa Rei Shrine. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1731 (Kyōhō 16), the fourth domain lord {{ill|Date Muratoki|ja|伊達村年}} ordered the shrine to be moved again to a part of the Kamegae Castle ruins, and it was relocated to its present site in 1735 (Kyōhō 20). In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == Ceremonies == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei Festival. == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming Location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines Festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Warei Shrine]][[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] ]]

It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. In 1615 (Genna 1), he followed Masamune's eldest son, {{ill|Date Hidemune|ja|伊達秀宗}}, when he was transferred to Uwajima. As a chief retainer of the Uwajima Domain, he supported the domain's administration. Uwajima had been impoverished by the previous lord's misrule, but Kiyomasa implemented tax reductions and industrial promotion, which had a positive effect. However, in 1620 (Genna 6), Hidemune, influenced by slander from other retainers, believed the accusations and had Kiyomasa and his sons killed ({{ill|Warei Incident|ja|和霊騒動}}). == History == The shrine is located at the northern end of Uwajima city, on the site of Kamegae Castle. The people who admired Kiyomasa secretly built a small shrine in the grounds of Yamen Koshin Shrine in Kitamori, where they enshrined the spirits of Kiyomasa and his family. Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. When it became clear that Kiyomasa was innocent, Hidemune established a shrine dedicated to Kiyomasa in 1653 (Jōō 2) and named it Yamayorikawa Rei Shrine. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1731 (Kyōhō 16), the fourth domain lord {{ill|Date Muratoki|ja|伊達村年}} ordered the shrine to be moved again to a part of the Kamegae Castle ruins, and it was relocated to its present site in 1735 (Kyōhō 20). In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == Ceremonies == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei Festival. == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming Location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines Festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Warei Shrine]][[Category:[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]

Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. When it became clear that Kiyomasa was innocent, Hidemune established a shrine dedicated to Kiyomasa in 1653 (Jōō 2) and named it Yamayorikawa Rei Shrine. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1731 (Kyōhō 16), the fourth domain lord {{ill|Date Muratoki|ja|伊達村年}} ordered the shrine to be moved again to a part of the Kamegae Castle ruins, and it was relocated to its present site in 1735 (Kyōhō 20). In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == Ceremonies == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei festival. == Local Information == ; location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Warei Shrine]][[Category:[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]

It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ;Main deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. In 1615 (Genna 1), he followed Masamune's eldest son, {{ill|Date Hidemune|ja|伊達秀宗}}, when he was transferred to Uwajima. As a chief retainer of the Uwajima Domain, he supported the domain's administration. Uwajima had been impoverished by the previous lord's misrule, but Kiyomasa implemented tax reductions and industrial promotion, which had a positive effect. However, in 1620 (Genna 6), Hidemune, influenced by slander from other retainers, believed the accusations and had Kiyomasa and his sons killed ({{ill|Warei Incident|ja|和霊騒動}}). == History == The shrine is located at the northern end of Uwajima city, on the site of Kamegae Castle. The people who admired Kiyomasa secretly built a small shrine in the grounds of Yamen Koshin Shrine in Kitamori, where they enshrined the spirits of Kiyomasa and his family. Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == ceremony == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei festival. == Local Information == ; location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Warei Shrine]][[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]

{{image request|Pre-war Zuishinmon|Pre-war main shrine|date=September 2016|cat=Uwajima City|cat2=History}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Warei Shrine |image=[[File:Warei Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px]]
main hall |location={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 |rank degrees=33 | rank minutes=13 | rank seconds=47 | N/S=N |Longitude degrees=132 |Longitude minutes=33 |Longitude seconds=55 | E/W=E |Country code=JP |map=Japan Ehime |Label position=left |deity={{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼|lt=Yamaga Seibei Kiyomasa}} |rank=Old {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|ja|近代rank制度}} Prefectural Shrine, {{ill|Special Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded={{ill|Jōō|ja|承応}} 2 (1653) |Main hall= |Other names= |annual festival={{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日}}・{{ill|July 24|ja|7月24日}} (Warei festival) |ceremony= }} '''Warei Shrine'''（われいじんじゃ{{ill|Warei Shrine|ja|和霊神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}. It is an old Prefectural Shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ;Main deity * {{ill|Yamaga Kiyomasa|ja|山家公頼}} (commonly known as Seibei) :Yamaga Kiyomasa was a retainer of {{ill|Date Masamune|ja|伊達政宗}}. In 1615 (Genna 1), he followed Masamune's eldest son, {{ill|Date Hidemune|ja|伊達秀宗}}, when he was transferred to Uwajima. As a chief retainer of the Uwajima Domain, he supported the domain's administration. Uwajima had been impoverished by the previous lord's misrule, but Kiyomasa implemented tax reductions and industrial promotion, which had a positive effect. However, in 1620 (Genna 6), Hidemune, influenced by slander from other retainers, believed the accusations and had Kiyomasa and his sons killed ({{ill|Warei Incident|ja|和霊騒動}}). == History == The shrine is located at the northern end of Uwajima city, on the site of Kamegae Castle. The people who admired Kiyomasa secretly built a small shrine in the grounds of Yamen Koshin Shrine in Kitamori, where they enshrined the spirits of Kiyomasa and his family. Afterwards, those involved in Kiyomasa's murder began to die suddenly from lightning strikes, marine accidents, and other strange occurrences. When it became clear that Kiyomasa was innocent, Hidemune established a shrine dedicated to Kiyomasa in 1653 (Jōō 2) and named it Yamayorikawa Rei Shrine. As the number of worshippers increased and the shrine grounds became too small, the shrine was moved to Mukoyama (within the present {{ill|Uwajima City Jonan Junior High School|ja|宇和島市立城北中学校}}), then to Moriyasu. In 1731 (Kyōhō 16), the fourth domain lord {{ill|Date Muratoki|ja|伊達村年}} ordered the shrine to be moved again to a part of the Kamegae Castle ruins, and it was relocated to its present site in 1735 (Kyōhō 20). In 1919 (Taisho 8), the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine. In 1953 (Showa 28), it was listed as a Special Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. The shrine was destroyed by air raids in 1945 (Showa 20) but was splendidly rebuilt in 1957 (Showa 32). Shrines enshrining the divided spirit of Warei Shrine can be found across Japan, mainly in the Shikoku and Chugoku regions. == Grounds == The shrine's forest was designated a Natural Monument by the city in 1974. == ceremony == On January 2nd and 3rd, there are offerings of iaijutsu (swordsmanship). The annual festival on July 23rd and 24th is called the Warei festival. == Local Information == ; location * {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Uwajima|ja|宇和島市}}, Warei Town 1451 ; Transportation Access * Nearest station: {{ill|Uwajima Station|ja|宇和島駅}} on the {{ill|Yosan Line|ja|予讃線}} operated by {{ill|JR Shikoku|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. About a 7-minute walk from the station. == As a Filming location == * The movie "{{ill|Wolf Fire of the South Sea|ja|南海の狼火}}" (1960, Nikkatsu), starring {{ill|Akira Kobayashi|ja|小林旭}} was filmed here. == Connection with Sendai == The descendants of Kiyomasa's eldest son, Kiyobei, who remained in Sendai, include Yamataka Toyozaburo (Yorimichi), who enshrined the divided spirit from the Warei Shrine in Uwajima as "Yamataka Myojin" in his residence on Higashi Ichibancho. Later, "Yamataka Myojin" was renamed "Warei Shrine", and in 1883 (Meiji 16), the shrine was relocated to the premises at the corner of Higashi Nibancho and Tatemachi Streets. During the air raid on Sendai in July 1945 (Showa 20), the shrine was completely burned down, leaving only two guardian dog statues. In 1973 (Showa 48), the shrine was relocated to Taihaku (present {{ill|Aoba-ku, Sendai|ja|青葉区台原}}) along with the relocation of the Yamataka family. In 1977 (Showa 52), following the demand from Ichibancho shopping street, which had previously hosted the "Yamataka Myojin", the shrine was re-enshrined on the roof of the present-day {{ill|Sendai Forus|ja|仙台フォーラス}} shopping complex. Every July, as one of the "Ichibancho Three Shrines festival," the mikoshi of Warei Shrine parades through the central shopping streets of Sendai along with those of the {{ill|Fujisaki Ebisu Shrine|ja|藤崎えびす神社}} and Nonaka Shrine. == Warei Belief == Worshipped mainly in the Chugoku and Shikoku regions, as of 1982, there are 159 branch shrines across 9 prefectures in western Japan (including 82 in Ehime Prefecture and 26 in Okayama Prefecture) and one in the Tohoku region (two in Sendai City). The earliest example of the worship being introduced is the Warei Shrine in Rokutanji, Shimanto Town, Kochi Prefecture (1737), and Warei Shrine in Hokaiji Temple in Tamagawa Town, Imabari City, Ehime Prefecture (1746). The former is believed to be the place where Kiyomasa's wife Sumi died, and both Kiyomasa and his wife Sumi are enshrined there. The latter shrine was established following a divine message suggesting worship at Warei Shrine to cure illness, with local landlord Yokari Yomemon becoming a worshipper after his illness was completely cured. Initially, it was an auxiliary shrine within Mishima Shrine (a branch of Oyamazumi Shrine), but as the number of worshippers increased, it became an independent shrine. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Warei Shrine (Kochi City)|ja|和霊神社 (高知市)}} - A shrine in Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture. The fourth-generation ancestor of {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} enshrined the spirit from Warei Shrine. * {{ill|Marunouchi Warei Shrine|ja|丸之内和霊神社}} - A shrine on the site of Kiyomasa's residence. * {{ill|Akio Toru|ja|明石虎雄}} - A local architect from Uwajima who designed, supervised, and constructed the shrine's worship hall. == References == === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="annotations"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commons|Category:Warei-jinja}} * [http://www.uwajima.org/spot/index3.html Uwajima City Tourism Association (Warei Shrine)] * [http://ehime-jinjacho.jp/jinja/?p=444 Warei Shrine - Ehime Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:われいしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Old Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Uwajima City]] [[Category:History of Uwajima City]] [[Category:Uwajima Domain]] [[Category:Yamaga Clan]] [[Category:Municipal Natural Monuments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Warei Shrine]][[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Warei Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]
